ground_truth.title: CS 224 Fall 23
ground_truth.main_content: Algorithms for Data Science CS 224, Fall 2023. This is a graduate-level topics class on algorithmic challenges arising in modern machine learning and data science more broadly. The course will touch upon a number of well-studied problems (generative modeling, deep learning theory, adversarial robustness, inverse problems, inference) and frameworks for algorithm design (gradient descent, spectral methods, tensor/moment methods, message passing, convex programming hierarchies, Markov chains). Time/Location: MW 2:15-3:30, SEC LL2-224. Instructor: Sitan Chen (sitan@seas.harvard.edu). Office hours: SEC 3.325, Thursday 4-5. Teaching Fellows: David Brewster, Depen Morwani, Rosie Zhao with their respective office hours. Course covers topics including Tensors, Sum-of-squares, Robust statistics, Supervised learning, Computational complexity, and Statistical physics with associated lecture notes and resources. Student projects include work on latent variable models, algebraic circuit reconstruction, diffusion models, and mean-field approximation. Assignments include problem sets with PDF downloads and a scribing template.
